context: Tests whether the agent correctly applies UIKit memory management patterns when implementing a view controller that uses a Timer, CADisplayLink, NotificationCenter, a delegate, and an async Task. Grading is based on the Swift source files and the memory_analysis.md produced by the agent.

checklist.name: [weak self] in Timer closure
checklist.description: Timer is created using the block-based API (scheduledTimer(withTimeInterval:repeats:block:)) and the closure captures self with [weak self]

checklist.name: Timer invalidated in viewWillDisappear
checklist.description: timer?.invalidate() (or equivalent) is called inside an override of viewWillDisappear(_:), NOT in deinit or viewDidDisappear

checklist.name: CADisplayLink weak proxy
checklist.description: CADisplayLink is connected to self via a WeakProxy (or equivalent weak-reference wrapper class) rather than directly referencing self as the target

checklist.name: NotificationCenter [weak self]
checklist.description: The NotificationCenter block-based observer (addObserver(forName:object:queue:using:)) captures self with [weak self] inside the handler closure

checklist.name: Observer removed in deinit
checklist.description: The NotificationCenter observer token is passed to NotificationCenter.default.removeObserver(_:) inside deinit, NOT in viewWillDisappear or viewDidDisappear

checklist.name: Delegate declared weak
checklist.description: The delegate property is declared as 'weak var delegate: <ProtocolName>?' (not strong)

checklist.name: Delegate protocol AnyObject
checklist.description: The delegate protocol declaration includes ': AnyObject' as a constraint (or equivalent class constraint)

checklist.name: Task stored as property
checklist.description: The async Task for the network fetch is stored in a stored property (e.g. 'private var fetchTask: Task<Void, Never>?') rather than being fire-and-forget

checklist.name: Task cancelled in viewDidDisappear
checklist.description: The stored Task is cancelled by calling .cancel() inside an override of viewDidDisappear(_:), NOT in deinit

checklist.name: Task.isCancelled checked after await
checklist.description: After each await expression inside the Task, there is a guard or check for Task.isCancelled (or Task.checkCancellation()) before any UI update

checklist.name: No Task.detached for UI work
checklist.description: Does NOT use Task.detached for code that updates the UI — uses Task {} (which inherits actor isolation) or explicit await MainActor.run {} instead

checklist.name: deinit logs deallocation
checklist.description: The view controller has a deinit implementation that calls print() or equivalent to log that the instance was deallocated (e.g. print("\(type(of: self)) deallocated") or similar)
